---
title: Using the utility
subtitle: Clipping and stitching GoPro videos from SEFIS video surveys
metadata-files:
  - ../_metadata.yml
order: 4
---

There are multiple ways to set up and use this utility, depending on your familiarity with Git, R, Python, and/or command line. In order of increasing technical complexity, the options are:

### No command line

The easiest way to launch the utility on Windows is to use the `process_videos.bat` file. You will be prompted to select the configurations YAML file to use, and the script will run according to the settings specified in that file.

Alternatively, the `process_videos_dragndrop.bat` file will open a Windows Command Prompt terminal and prompt you to drag and drop the configurations YAML file into the terminal. Open a File Explorer window, navigate to the desired YAML file, and drag it into the terminal. The script will then run according to the settings specified in that file.

### RStudio and R

For users comfortable using R and RStudio, the `process_videos.R` script can be run directly from RStudio. Open the project in RStudio and run the `process_videos.R` script. You will be prompted to select the configurations YAML file to use, and the script will run according to the settings specified in that file.

::: {.callout-note title="A note for R users"}
The `process_videos.R` script is a launcher/wrapper script that runs the Python video processing utility `process_videos.py`. It is pre-configured to use the 'Private Library' (Virtual Environment) created during setup. You don't need to manually activate anything; just double-click the Rscript file and provide your configuration file when prompted.
:::

### Python and command line

Advanced users comfortable with command line and Python can run the `process_videos.py` script directly from the command line. Open a command line terminal and navigate to the project directory. Activate the Python virtual environment [created at setup](setup.qmd#python-and-command-line):

```{.bash filename="pip environment in Windows Command Prompt"}
.venv\Scripts\activate
```
   
```{.bash filename="pip environment in Git Bash"}
source .venv/Scripts/activate
```
   
```{.bash filename="pip environment in Linux or Mac Terminal"}
source .venv/bin/activate
```

```{.bash filename="conda environment"}
conda activate video-processing-venv
```

Then execute the `process_videos.py` script, passing the path to the desired configurations YAML file as an argument:

```bash
python process_videos.py path/to/config.yaml
```

No other arguments are needed. The script will run according to the settings specified in the YAML file.

When finished, deactivate the virtual environment:

```{.bash filename="pip environment"}
deactivate
```

```{.bash filename="conda environment"}
conda deactivate
```
